data.description:
## Why

A cluster of low-severity hygiene items: a confirmed dead parallel implementation
(~212 LOC), a few latent traps, and several small consistency/correctness-of-docs
fixes. None is urgent, but together they reduce noise for the next auditor and
close two latent foot-guns. Grouped into one "cleanups" story so they can be done
in a single low-risk pass (or cherry-picked).

## What

Delete confirmed dead code; make `reduce_all_tickets`'s boolean params
keyword-only; introduce shared HTTP-status constants/predicates in the reconciler;
sort `__all__`s and fix the `attach_commits` omission; and correct two stale
internal annotations (the M-5 global doc-note and the M-2 cycle note).

## Scope

- In scope: the specific sites below. Each item is independent.
- Out of scope: anything that changes public behavior; the larger reconciler
  refactors (S7); the documentation sweep (S3).

## Context (citations)

- **Dead parallel impl (delete):** `src/rebar/_engine_support/bridge_fsck.py:168,
  210,257,337` — `enumerate_{stale,duplicate,open_count_skew,orphan}_anomalies`,
  ~212 LOC total (lines 168-379). **0 references** (Serena `find_referencing_symbols`
  + grep over `src`/`tests`/`docs`; the only hit is a self-referential string
  literal at **line 303**, not 284). The live entry is `audit_bridge_mappings`
  (referenced from `__init__.py:916,919`). The block self-documents the deadness
  (≈271-273: "NOT on any live path … kept for the anomaly contract / tests") and one
  function does an in-process `from rebar_reconciler import acli` (**line 293**, not
  274) that **always** raises ModuleNotFoundError in-process (the reconciler lives
  at `rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler`).
- **Boolean trap (make keyword-only):** `src/rebar/reducer/_api.py` —
  `reduce_all_tickets` (def at ≈:179; bools at 181-183:
  `exclude_archived=False, exclude_deleted=False, exclude_session_logs=False`) has 3
  consecutive positional bools. All ~11 call sites already pass them by keyword, so
  adding a `*` is safe and prevents a future `reduce_all_tickets(t, True, False, True)`.
- **HTTP-status literals (name them):** bare `400/404/410/429/403` with
  *inconsistent* access across the reconciler — `exc.code`
  (`apply_outbound.py:215`, `acli_cli_ops.py:134`, `outbound_differ.py:107`,
  `applier.py:678`, `fetcher.py:341`, `acli_graph.py:238`) vs
  `getattr(exc,"status_code",None)` (`apply_outbound.py:415,440`,
  `batch_dispatch.py:511`) vs `resp.status` (`inbound_probe.py:106`);
  `batch_dispatch.py:300` even hedges both. No `http.HTTPStatus` import exists.
- **`__all__` hygiene:** 15 RUF022 unsorted `__all__` (cosmetic, ruff-fixable);
  `attach_commits` is public (`src/rebar/__init__.py:609`) and used in tests but is
  **absent** from `rebar.__all__` (≈979-1036).
- **Stale internal annotations:** the M-5 `_CLI_OVERRIDES` global
  (`config.py:410`) is fine under MCP (it is only ever set from the CLI entry
  `_cli/__init__.py:1017`, never from `mcp_server.py`) — the prior audit overstated
  a race; add a one-line comment recording that it is CLI-only so it isn't
  re-flagged. The M-2 reducer↔`_engine_support` lazy-import coupling is
  **asymmetric**, not a tidy mutual cycle: `_processors.py:214` imports
  `resolve_ticket_id` and *has* the "local import avoids a module-load circular dep"
  comment; `_engine_support/resolver.py:33` imports `rebar.reducer._alias.compute_alias`
  and has **no** such comment. The cleanup is just to add the explanatory comment on
  `resolver.py:33` (so the next auditor doesn't re-flag the lazy import as a smell).

## Implementation plan

1. **Delete** the four `enumerate_*` functions in `bridge_fsck.py` (lines
   ≈168-379) and the now-orphaned string literal at 284 if it only labeled them.
   Re-run Serena + grep to confirm zero references remain; run the bridge tests
   (`tests/scripts/bridge/**`) to confirm `audit_bridge_mappings` is unaffected.
2. **`reduce_all_tickets`:** insert a `*,` before `exclude_archived` in the
   signature (`reducer/_api.py:181`). Run the full suite — all call sites already
   use keywords, so this should be green; fix any positional caller surfaced.
3. **HTTP status:** add `from http import HTTPStatus` and small predicates
   (`_is_not_found(exc)`, `_is_gone(exc)`) in one reconciler util location that
   normalize the `.code` / `status_code` / `.status` access; replace the scattered
   literals/access forms with the predicates/constants. This also removes the
   `.code`-vs-`status_code` inconsistency (a latent bug). Run the reconciler tier.
4. **`__all__`:** run `ruff check --select RUF022 --fix src` to sort; add
   `attach_commits` to `rebar.__all__` (or mark it private if that is preferred —
   but it is tested as public, so add it).
5. **Annotations:** add the one-line CLI-only comment near `config.py:410`; add the
   symmetric circular-dep comment on `resolver.py:33`.
6. Run `ruff check src tests`, the scoped mypy gate, and the affected suites.

## Tradeoffs

- **Benefit:** removes ~212 LOC of dead code an editor could mistake for live;
  closes two latent foot-guns (positional bools, `.code`/`status_code` drift);
  stops the next audit from re-flagging the (benign) global and cycle.
- **Cost/risk:** the `enumerate_*` functions are module-public, so deletion is a
  *theoretical* breaking change for an out-of-tree importer — but none exists in
  this repo and they are self-labeled dead. The HTTP-predicate change touches many
  reconciler sites; do it as one reviewed pass with the reconciler tests as
  guardrail. Everything else is trivial.
- **Note:** do NOT "clean up" the reconciler `applier.py`/`acli.py` `__all__`
  entries — they deliberately re-export private handlers for the dispatch tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The four `bridge_fsck.enumerate_*` functions are deleted; `audit_bridge_mappings`
  and the bridge tests still pass; Serena/grep confirm no dangling references.
- [ ] `reduce_all_tickets`'s three booleans are keyword-only (`*` added); full suite green.
- [ ] Reconciler HTTP-status checks use shared constants/predicates with one
  consistent access form; reconciler tier passes.
- [ ] All `__all__`s are sorted (RUF022 clean) and `attach_commits` is in
  `rebar.__all__`.
- [ ] `config.py`'s `_CLI_OVERRIDES` carries a "CLI-only, not set under MCP" note;
  `resolver.py:33` carries the symmetric circular-dep note.
- [ ] ruff + mypy + affected suites stay green.
</content>
data.title: Dead-code removal + low-severity hygiene cleanups
